message:
Update skill generation to reflect flattened documentation structure (#166)

The agent skill now organizes documentation by category at the root level
(guides/, tutorials/, explanations/, reference/, integrations/) instead of
using a nested references/ directory. This improves agent navigation and
aligns with the Agent Skills specification.

Changes:
- Rename referencesDir to contentDir for clarity
- Update URL rewriting to use relative paths without prefix directory
- Update documentation guide with the new structure
- Update CLAUDE.md to reflect generated file organization

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -91,7 +91,7 @@ Some content is auto-generated and excluded from linting:
   `src/sidebar-ocsf.generated.ts`, `src/ocsf-version.mjs`.
 - **Agent Skill**: Run `bun run build:skill` to generate the Tenzir agent skill.
   Requires `bun run build:full` first to generate per-page markdown files in `dist/`.
-  Generated files: `tenzir/SKILL.md`, `tenzir/references/`.
+  Generated files: `tenzir/SKILL.md`, `tenzir/<doc-hierarchy>/`.
 
 ### Linting

@@ -48,18 +48,21 @@ The skill uses progressive disclosure to optimize context usage:
 ```
 tenzir/
 ├── SKILL.md        # Condensed sitemap with excerpts (~8,500 tokens)
-└── references/     # Full documentation hierarchy
+├── guides/         # Full documentation by category
+├── tutorials/
+├── explanations/
+├── reference/
+└── integrations/
 ```
 
 **SKILL.md** contains a navigable overview with:
 
 - Section descriptions (Guides, Tutorials, Explanations, Reference, Integrations)
 - Category headings with brief descriptions
-- Links to full documentation in `references/`
+- Links to full documentation pages
 
-**references/** mirrors the documentation site structure, containing the
-complete content of every page. Your agent can follow links from `SKILL.md` to
-load specific pages as needed.
+The documentation hierarchy sits alongside `SKILL.md` at the root level. Your
+agent can follow links from `SKILL.md` to load specific pages as needed.
 
 Every release is validated in CI using
 [skills-ref](https://github.com/agentskills/agentskills/tree/main/skills-ref)
@@ -75,7 +78,7 @@ The general approach is:
 
 1. Point your agent to the `tenzir/` directory as a skill source
 2. The agent reads `SKILL.md` first for an overview
-3. When the agent needs details, it follows links into `references/`
+3. When the agent needs details, it follows relative links to specific pages
 
 ### Example: Claude Code custom instructions
